directions.name: Phased 64x384 micro-panels to shrink the live set
directions.hypothesis: Keep the autotune-selected 64x384 outer CTA, but stop carrying the full 12-wide B and accumulator live set through the hot loop. The latest regressed run still sits at 126 registers/thread, occupancy limit 2, and only 32.95% active warps with tensor active down at 28.22%, so the best next move on the restored accepted base is to serialize the 384-wide work into smaller internal micro-panels such as 2x192 or 3x128 while preserving the 384-wide CTA-count advantage.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy and weak latency hiding from the 12-fragment live set in the hot 64x384 loop.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:32-39 (TileConfig shape parameters, especially kWarpMmaTilesN and kTensorBlockN)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:399-445 (warp tile mapping plus accumulator and B-fragment residency in the hot loop)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:517-550 (fixed-shape launch path that should keep the 64x384 outer tile and 64x96 tail split)
directions.risk: Serial micro-panels can give back some of the 64x384 CTA-count win if extra panel boundaries, reloads, or control flow add too much instruction overhead.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if registers/thread stays near 126 and occupancy remains capped at 2 resident CTAs, or if runtime regresses without a clear rise in active warps and tensor activity.

directions.name: Simplify the 64x384 K-loop pipeline instead of repacking B
directions.hypothesis: Round 1 is explicit negative evidence against a heavier two-level B repack: runtime regressed from the accepted 37.285807 ms result to 42.341888 ms while tensor active fell and both mio_throttle and barrier stall worsened. With the implementation surface restored to the accepted single-skew base, the next distinct lever is to reduce K-loop orchestration cost by simplifying stage management, copy participation, or wait/barrier placement rather than adding more B-layout machinery.
directions.expected_bottleneck: CTA-wide synchronization and copy-pipeline issue pressure in the hot loop.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:53-60 (current stage-count decision coupled to shared-memory budget)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:415-450 (cp.async prefill, commit/wait sequence, and CTA barriers in the hot loop)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:392-404 (warp-role derivation that could support a lighter producer/consumer schedule)
directions.risk: Reducing or specializing the pipeline can underfeed MMA if overlap gets worse, and warp-role changes can increase implementation complexity quickly.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if barrier stall and mio_throttle stay roughly flat after the pipeline change, or if reduced synchronization comes with lower tensor activity and no runtime gain.

directions.name: Trim the 64x384 epilogue export path on the restored single-skew base
directions.hypothesis: Since the heavier B repack already failed, the remaining non-repacking pressure point is the export path after MMA. The hot kernel still allocates about 50 KB of shared memory per block and routes every 16x16 accumulator tile through c_shared before BF16 vector stores, while L1/LSU utilization remains high in the regressed run. Revisit the c_shared staging and warp export pattern to remove shared round-trips or cheaper conversion/store sequencing without touching the accepted single-skew B layout.
directions.expected_bottleneck: LSU/shared writeback pressure and epilogue-side shared-memory traffic after the main MMA loop.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:41-46 (per-warp c_shared scratch sizing inside TileConfig)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:388-390 (shared allocation for the epilogue export buffer)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:201-205 (BF16 pair store helper) and src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:454-485 (store_matrix_sync plus BF16 export loop)
directions.risk: The epilogue path is correctness-sensitive and any attempt to bypass or compress the shared export step can introduce layout bugs or erase the benefit with extra conversion logic.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if LSU/shared-write metrics do not fall or if the export rewrite increases runtime or causes correctness drift while the hot-loop bottlenecks remain unchanged.

notes: Round 2/5 diagnosis prepared from regressed run 20260419_093633_bf16_gemm_v1_d90a873. Round-1 two-level B staging is treated as negative evidence; recommendation pivots to the human phased-64x384 idea on the restored accepted base surface.
